steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content: There are $31$ daily temperatures, so the median is the $\frac{31+1}{2}=16$th value when the data are ordered.

steps.title: Use cumulative frequencies
steps.content: Adding the frequencies from the lowest interval upward places values $1$ through $10$ below $25^\circ\text{F}$, and values $11$ through $16$ in the $25$ to $34$ interval. Therefore, the 16th value is in the $25$ to $34$ interval.

steps.title: List possible medians
steps.content: The recorded temperatures are integers, so the median could be any integer from $25$ through $34$: 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, or 34.
